Tier 3 â€” Local real-time delivery. Gas peakers are marginal-cost units dispatched when baseload (nuclear/hydro) is insufficient. Output >3,000 MW = demand stress; >5,000 MW = grid under significant pressure. Elevated gas dispatch correlates with higher wholesale electricity prices and increased exposure to Tier 1/2 commodity risk. Hour ending 24:00 UTC.
results.threshold_note: Gas peakers elevated â€” demand stress signal

Futures price (~15 min delay). Tier 1 - Global commodity. ICE Brent crude: global benchmark for ~2/3 of world oil trade. Ontario supply chain: Brent (Tier 1) -> TCPL pipeline utilization (Tier 2) -> Toronto pump price / IESO gas dispatch (Tier 3). CAD/USD amplifies or dampens pass-through to Ontario consumers. Warn >$80/bbl (refinery margin pressure), Alert >$100/bbl (Hormuz disruption threshold). Source: Yahoo Finance ICE Brent front-month futures (BZ=F), near-real-time.

Enbridge Dawn: No capacity constraints on all monitored paths. Distribution areas: 76G/0Y/0R across 76 path-days (4-day outlook). Last updated: June 08, 2026. Key S&T paths for GTA supply: Dawnâ†’Parkway (primary corridor), Dawnâ†’Kirkwall, Kirkwallâ†’Parkway. States: Green=No constraints, Yellow=Interruptible impacted, Red=Firm services impacted. Signals: Traffic signal TDs: 76 all-green.

Dawn + Tecumseh working storage: 120 PJ (113.7 Bcf, 38.1% of 315 PJ working capacity). Previous period: 153 PJ (145.0 Bcf). Change: -33 PJ (â–Ľ withdrawing). Season: injection season (Aprâ€“Sep) â€” post-winter refill underway. Thresholds â€” Warn: <63 PJ, Alert: <32 PJ. Ontario gas supply chain: Brent crude (Tier 1) â†’ TCPL pipelines (Tier 2) â†’ Dawn storage + Enbridge distribution (Tier 2) â†’ GTA consumers (Tier 3). Dawn is Canada's largest integrated underground storage facility (~33 pools). Published twice monthly. Source: https://www.enbridgegas.com/storage-transportation/operational-information/storage-reporting
results.threshold_note: Storage below 142 PJ (45% capacity) â€” reduced winter buffer
